entries.title: Abelian Group
entries.content: A group in which the binary operation is commutative, meaning the order of elements does not affect the result.
entries.example: Integers under addition (2 + 3 = 3 + 2)

entries.title: Absolute Value
entries.content: The non-negative distance of a real number from zero on the number line, regardless of its sign.

entries.title: Acute Angle
entries.content: An angle that measures strictly between 0 and 90 degrees (or between 0 and pi/2 radians).
entries.example: An angle of 45 degrees in a right triangle

entries.title: Addition Property
entries.content: Adding the same quantity to both sides of an equation maintains the equality.
entries.formula: If a = b, then a + c = b + c
entries.example: If x - 3 = 5, then x = 8

entries.title: Adjacency Matrix
entries.content: A square matrix used to represent a finite graph, showing whether pairs of vertices are adjacent or not.
entries.example: A 3x3 matrix showing connections in a triangle graph

entries.title: Algebraic Expression
entries.content: A mathematical phrase that can contain numbers, operators, and variables, but no equals sign.

entries.title: Algorithm
entries.content: A step-by-step procedure or set of rules to solve a specific mathematical or computational problem.
entries.formula: Step 1: Input x. Step 2: Return x * x.
entries.example: Euclidean algorithm for greatest common divisor

entries.title: Alternate Interior Angles
entries.content: A pair of angles on opposite sides of a transversal line and between the two parallel lines it intersects.
entries.example: Angles formed when a diagonal line crosses parallel train tracks

entries.title: Amplitude
entries.content: The maximum displacement of a periodic wave or function from its equilibrium or mean value.
entries.example: Amplitude of y = 3sin(x) is 3

entries.title: Angle Bisector
entries.content: A line or ray that divides an angle into two equal, adjacent angles.
entries.example: Dividing a 90-degree angle into two 45-degree angles

entries.title: Antiderivative
entries.content: A function whose derivative is the original given function. Also known as the indefinite integral.
entries.example: Antiderivative of 2x is x^2 + C

entries.title: Apex of Cone
entries.content: The highest point or tip of a cone, opposite to the base.
entries.example: The sharp tip of an ice cream cone

entries.title: Apothem
entries.content: A line segment from the center of a regular polygon to the midpoint of one of its sides.
entries.example: Apothem of a regular hexagon with side length 4

entries.title: Arc Length
entries.content: The distance along a curved line segment forming all or part of a circle or curve.
entries.example: Arc length of a semicircle with radius 5 is 5*pi

entries.title: Area of Circle
entries.content: The total two-dimensional space enclosed by a circle of a given radius.
entries.example: Area of circle with radius 3 is 9*pi

entries.title: Arithmetic Mean
entries.content: The average of a set of numbers, calculated by dividing their sum by the total count.
entries.example: Mean of 2, 4, 6 is 4

entries.title: Arithmetic Progression
entries.content: A sequence of numbers in which the difference between consecutive terms is constant.
entries.example: Sequence: 3, 7, 11, 15... with d = 4

entries.title: Associative Property
entries.content: The grouping of numbers does not affect the result of addition or multiplication.

entries.title: Asymptote
entries.content: A line that a curve approaches infinitely closely but never actually meets or crosses.
entries.formula: limit as x approaches infinity of f(x) = L
entries.example: Horizontal asymptote of y = 1/x is y = 0

entries.title: Augmented Matrix
entries.content: A matrix obtained by appending the columns of two matrices, typically for solving systems of linear equations.
entries.example: Augmenting coefficients matrix with constants vector

entries.title: Average Rate of Change
entries.content: The change in y divided by the change in x over a specific interval of a function.
entries.example: Average speed over a trip

entries.title: Axiom
entries.content: A statement or proposition that is assumed to be true without proof, serving as a premise for further reasoning.
entries.example: Parallel postulate in geometry

entries.title: Axis of Symmetry
entries.content: A line that divides a shape or graph into two symmetric, mirror-image halves.
entries.example: Axis of symmetry of y = x^2 is x = 0

entries.title: Adjacent Side
entries.content: The side of a right triangle that is next to the reference angle, but is not the hypotenuse.
entries.example: Adjacent side of 30-degree angle in 3-4-5 triangle

entries.title: Affine Transformation
entries.content: A geometric transformation that preserves collinearity and ratios of distances, though not necessarily angles or lengths.
entries.example: Translation and rotation of coordinates

entries.title: Alternating Series
entries.content: An infinite series whose terms alternate between positive and negative signs.

entries.title: Angle of Depression
entries.content: The angle between the horizontal line of sight and the object below the horizontal line.
entries.example: Looking down from a lighthouse at a boat

entries.title: Angle of Elevation
entries.content: The angle between the horizontal line of sight and the object above the horizontal line.
entries.example: Looking up at the top of a tree

entries.title: Annulus Area
entries.content: The area of the ring-shaped region bounded by two concentric circles of different radii.
entries.example: Annulus with outer radius 5 and inner radius 3

entries.title: Approximation Error
entries.content: The difference between an approximate value and the exact, true value.
entries.example: Approximating pi as 3.14 has error 0.00159

entries.title: Argument of Complex Number
entries.content: The angle between the positive real axis and the vector representing the complex number in the complex plane.
entries.example: Argument of 1 + i is 45 degrees

entries.title: Arithmetic Series
entries.content: The sum of the terms of an arithmetic progression sequence.

entries.title: Automorphism
entries.content: An isomorphism from a mathematical object or structure onto itself.
entries.example: Identity map on a vector space

entries.title: Auxiliary Equation
entries.content: An algebraic equation used to find the general solution of a homogeneous linear differential equation.
entries.example: Solving y'' - 5y' + 6y = 0

entries.title: Average Deviation
entries.content: The average of the absolute differences between each data point and the mean of the data set.
entries.example: Average deviation of 1, 2, 9 is 3.55

entries.title: Axiomatic System
entries.content: A set of axioms from which theorems can be logically derived using rules of inference.
entries.example: Hilbert's axiomatic geometry system

entries.title: Abscissa
entries.content: The horizontal coordinate of a point in a two-dimensional Cartesian coordinate system.
entries.example: For point (4, 7), the abscissa is 4

entries.title: Absolute Convergence
entries.content: An infinite series converges absolutely if the series of absolute values converges.
entries.example: Sum of (-1)^n / n^2 converges absolutely

entries.title: Accumulation Point
entries.content: A point x is an accumulation point of a set S if every neighborhood of x contains a point in S distinct from x.
entries.example: 0 is an accumulation point of {1/n : n in N}

entries.title: Algebraic Geometry
entries.content: A branch of mathematics studying geometry using techniques from abstract algebra, particularly coordinate rings.
entries.formula: V(I) = {x in k^n : f(x) = 0 for all f in I}
entries.example: Studying curves defined by polynomial equations
